Elira Veythari: graceful half-elf figure, slightly taller than human, with subtly pointed ears and a lean, sinewy build, silver hair cropped short to jagged spikes, framing sharp cheekbones and a jagged scar running from temple to jawline, gold eyes glinting beneath a high collar of reinforced plate armor etched with Tower Authority sigils, wielding a heavy crossbow slung across her back and a longsword strapped to her thigh, one hand gripping a data pad with cracked holographic screens, her posture rigid as she scans the dimly lit archives of the Spire District, where bioluminescent fungi cling to rusted metal beams and the air hums with static from ancient machinery.
Kaelan Veyth: a towering dragonborn figure, obsidian-scaled arms scarred with faint glowing runes, amber eyes flickering like dying embers, draped in a frayed charcoal cloak over a gunmetal-and-burgundy coat with a subtle iron fang mark on the lapel, a concealed dagger sheathed at the hip and a crossbow slung across the chest, their weathered hands gripping a rusted chain linked to a stolen relic humming with unstable energy.
Klikk Scrapweaver: hunched goblin figure, 3-foot-tall, wiry frame draped in ash-white archival robes frayed at the edges, skin a dusky charcoal with ink-stained knuckles, ash-gray hair tangled and streaked with soot, wide eyes glazed with a permanent film of ash from handling cursed scrolls, patchwork cloak stitched from torn spellscrolls draped over chainmail vest and scale-mail bracers, warhammer slung across broad shoulders and crossbow holstered at hip, one ear flicking toward a faint whisper of ancient parchment unraveling in the vaults below.
Veyra Duskhollow: A tall, lithe elf with silver hair streaked ash-black, their amber eyes glowing faintly like smoldering embers, their ageless face etched with the wear of countless bargains; they wear a patchwork duster of reinforced leather and scaled fabric, its sleeves frayed at the edges, layered over a tunic of faded midnight blue, the fabric stitched with faint, glowing runes that pulse like a heartbeat; a curved dagger rests at their hip, its blade etched with shifting constellations, and a satchel slung across their shoulder spills out shards of shattered crystal and rolled parchment, while a single silver pendant—a twisted key—hangs around their neck, its surface etched with a map of forgotten ruins.
Elias Vorne: Gaunt human frame, pale skin streaked with soot and ink, dark hair plastered to his skull by smog, eyes a sharp amber hue glinting beneath a hooded robe fraying at the cuffs, layered over chainmail etched with faded arcane symbols; a rusted crossbow slung across his back, warhammer gripped in calloused hands, one ink-stained finger tracing a cracked mirror fragment tucked into his belt, its fractured surface reflecting the flicker of dying lanterns above.
Seraphine Duskveil: A tall, lithe Aasimar figure, 6'2", their radiant skin shimmering with faint golden undertones, left eye pulsing with sickly gold light while starlight veins trace their temples, clad in ash-gray chainmail over a patched charcoal cloak stained with soot, their Warden badge glinting on the chest, a cigarette burns between calloused fingers though the smoke vanishes into the air, ash mark etched on the left cheek, spear and crossbow slung across the back, handaxe strapped to the thigh, the faint glow of their eye casting an eerie light across the Smoldering Stairwell.
Kaelen Veyth: towering Dragonborn figure, obsidian scales shimmering with faint golden coiled serpent patterns, a jagged scar etching from cheek to chin, eyes glinting like fractured amber beneath a hooded, hunched posture; jade-green silk robes frayed at the edges, embroidered with writhing serpents and pendant seals glinting black as onyx, tattered woolen coat draped over one shoulder, ink-stained fingers clutching a quill that glints like a dagger, forked tongue flickering past cracked lips as they pore over parchment stained with faded blood.
Kaelen Duskhorn: a diminutive kobold, 2.5 feet tall, with dappled rust-colored scales and a long, sinuous tail coiled tightly around a gnarled wrist, amber eyes sharp and unblinking beneath a fringe of frizzled black hair, clad in grime-stained leather and chainmail bearing the tarnished silver sigil of the Adventurers Guild, a cracked hauberk draped over one shoulder and a crossbow slung across his chest, its strap frayed from decades of use, the faint scent of sulfur clinging to his skin from failed exorcisms.
Ignacia Vitreous: slender frame, standing just over five feet tall, with skin the hue of smoldering embers and violet-gray undertones, her crimson horns etched with delicate, frosted glass patterns, tail ending in a cracked, opalescent gemstone tip that glints faintly in the dim light; pale blue scholar robes layered over a soot-stained leather apron, sleeves frayed at the cuffs, ink-stained fingers gripping a brass-armed scroll case, one eye obscured by a glass-lens goggles with a cracked lens, reinforced elbow pads scuffed with dried ink, and a jagged, blood-smeared dagger strapped to her thigh beneath the robes.
Elara Veyne: a human of average height and wiry build, her pale skin etched with soot-smudged freckles and a jagged scar slicing her left eye, dark hair matted and streaked with ash, her charcoal-gray cloak frayed at the hem and pinned with a rusted Wizards Tower badge, chainmail sleeves peeking beneath leather gloves as she grips a weathered warhammer, one hand clutching a vial of glowing crimson liquid that leaks a faint trail of vapor behind her.
Pip Wrenthorn: wiry halfling frame, barely three feet tall, lithe and sinewy, perched on the balls of his feet, pale skin etched with ink stains and a jagged scar tracing from temple to jaw, ash-blonde hair streaked with soot framing large, amber eyes that flicker with wary intelligence, patchwork coat stitched from scavenged leather fraying at the edges, collar folded over a scarred collarbone, warhammer slung across one shoulder, crossbow strapped to his thigh, chainmail shirt worn thin in places revealing glimpses of scarred flesh, a single glowing arcane sigil embedded in his palm humming with stolen energy.
